name: Five Lakes Law Group
description_short: Five Lakes Law Group is a Michigan-based law firm specializing in debt settlement, founded in 2021. BBB A+ accredited since 2025. 4.8 Trustpilot, 6,000+ Google reviews. Fee rate reportedly 27% — higher than industry average.
description_long:
Five Lakes Law Group PLLC is a debt relief law firm headquartered in Southfield, Michigan, founded in 2021. Despite being relatively new, the firm has scaled rapidly, claiming to have served over 150,000 clients. Five Lakes earned BBB accreditation with an A+ rating in February 2025 and maintains strong review profiles across platforms — a 4.8-star Trustpilot rating from over 4,200 reviews and approximately 6,000 Google reviews. The firm's legal structure (PLLC) differentiates it from non-attorney debt settlement companies, potentially offering additional consumer protections including attorney-client privilege and the ability to provide legal defense if creditors pursue litigation.

The firm's primary service is attorney-backed debt settlement — negotiating with creditors to accept less than the full balance owed on unsecured debts including credit cards, medical bills, and personal loans. Like other debt settlement providers, the process typically involves clients stopping payments to creditors, accumulating funds in a dedicated account, and having the firm negotiate lump-sum settlements. Five Lakes operates under the FTC's fee-after-settlement model, meaning fees are only charged after a successful negotiation.

A significant concern raised in consumer reviews is the fee rate. Multiple BBB complaints cite a 27% fee on enrolled debt amounts — notably higher than the industry standard of 15-25%. On a $30,000 enrolled debt balance, that difference between 20% and 27% represents $2,100 in additional fees. The firm has 128 BBB complaints closed in the last three years, with common issues including fee disputes, settlement outcome expectations, and communication gaps. While the firm responds to complaints, only a portion result in consumer-satisfactory resolution. No CFPB enforcement actions or state AG actions appear in public records.

When evaluating debt relief companies, consumers should compare settlement programs against alternatives like debt consolidation loans, which combine multiple debts into a single fixed-rate payment. Credit counseling through nonprofit agencies offers free budgeting help without impacting credit scores. For those whose credit has already been damaged, credit repair services can address inaccurate negative items on reports. Personal loans for bad credit may provide funds for debt payoff at lower rates than credit cards, and credit monitoring services help track progress throughout the recovery process. Consolidating high-interest balances into a single installment loan with a fixed rate can reduce total interest paid and simplify monthly budgeting.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

certifications: BBB A+ rating, accredited since February 2025

pros: Attorney-backed representation provides stronger legal positioning than non-attorney debt settlement companies
Pro se legal document drafting and lawsuit defense included at no additional cost for all clients
Mobile app dashboard with real-time progress tracking, deposit viewing, and detailed FAQs
Creditor harassment assistance and debt collector wrongful conduct evaluation included
24/7 chat availability and extended phone support hours (7AM-9PM CT weekdays)
Claims to reduce monthly payments by 40% or more compared to minimum payments
Targets 24-48 month program completion with potential debt-free outcome
No interest rates or hidden fees explicitly stated
cons: Requires intentional payment diversion to creditors, which temporarily damages credit scores substantially during the program
Website does not disclose specific program fees, service costs, or how attorney fees are structured
The 24-48 month timeline and 40%+ payment reduction are presented without supporting data on actual client outcomes or success rates
Extent of actual attorney involvement versus paraprofessional support is not clearly defined
Privacy policy last updated January 2021, raising questions about current data protection practices
best_for: Consumers with $10,000+ in unsecured debt who want legal representation backing their debt settlement negotiations
Those who prefer the additional consumer protection of working with a law firm rather than a non-attorney debt settlement company
Individuals in financial hardship willing to accept credit score damage in exchange for negotiated debt reduction
Borrowers who have already been sued by creditors and need combined legal defense and settlement services
diagnosis: Five Lakes Law Group offers attorney-backed debt settlement which provides meaningful advantages — legal defense if sued, attorney-client privilege, and professional negotiation. The rapid growth (150,000 clients since 2021) and BBB A+ are positive signals. However, the reported 27% fee rate is significantly above the 15-25% industry norm and should be verified before enrollment. 128 BBB complaints in three years for a young firm warrants attention. Compare fees directly against TurboDebt, National Debt Relief, and Americor before committing. Best for consumers already facing creditor lawsuits who need combined legal defense and settlement services.
services: Debt consolidation with monthly payment reduction (claimed 40%+ reduction)
Attorney-backed debt settlement negotiations with creditors
1-on-1 ongoing support from licensed attorneys throughout program
Pro se legal document drafting at no additional cost
Creditor lawsuit defense and settlement assistance
Debt collector harassment evaluation and wrongful conduct assistance
Mobile app for progress monitoring, deposit tracking, and FAQ access
Debt management program with 24-48 month target completion timeline
24/7 chat support and extended phone support (855-441-6129)
Client dashboard and account login management
Credit impact counseling and timeline education
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
